mnemonics: Zombie Slayer: SIGCLD is a signal for cleaning up zombie processes (exited children), not for creating them.
keyPoints: SIGCLD is the System V name for the child status change signal, synonymous with SIGCHLD.
Its behavior historically varied: older System V systems would not queue the signal, potentially losing child exit notifications.
A process must explicitly wait() for its children to reap them and prevent zombies, even if a SIGCLD handler is installed.
commonMistakes: Assuming installing a SIGCLD/SIGCHLD handler automatically reaps zombie processes; you must call wait() inside the handler.
Confusing SIGCLD with other signals meant for process termination (like SIGKILL or SIGTERM); it is informational only.
Forgetting that the default action for SIGCLD is to be ignored, which is why zombies accumulate if the parent doesn't wait().
